data.description:

data.title: Darden Restaurants Approves Special Equity Grant for CEO
data.description:
Darden Restaurants ( (DRI) ) has provided an update.

On September 17, 2025, Darden Restaurants‘ Board approved a special equity grant for CEO Ricardo Cardenas, consisting of performance stock units with a target value of $17 million, set to vest in 2030 based on the company’s shareholder return relative to the S&P 500. Additionally, during the annual shareholder meeting held on the same day, nine directors were elected, executive compensation was approved, KPMG was ratified as the auditor, and a proposal on greenhouse gas emission targets was rejected.

data.title: SpartanNash Awaits Closure of Merger with New Mackinac
data.description:
SpartanNash Co ( (SPTN) ) just unveiled an update.

On June 22, 2025, SpartanNash Co entered into a Merger Agreement with New Mackinac HoldCo, Inc., Mackinac Merger Sub, Inc., and C&S Wholesale Grocers, LLC, which will result in SpartanNash becoming a wholly-owned subsidiary of New Mackinac HoldCo. The merger, subject to regulatory approval under the Hart-Scott-Rodino Antitrust Improvements Act, saw C&S resubmit its notification to the FTC, with the waiting period expiring on September 18, 2025, without FTC action. The transaction is expected to close around September 22, 2025, potentially impacting SpartanNash’s market operations and stakeholder interests.

data.title: WeRide Secures Belgium’s First Level 4 Autonomous Shuttle Permit
data.description:
WeRide ( (WRD) ) has provided an announcement.

WeRide announced on September 19, 2025, that its Robobus has received Belgium’s first federal test permit for a Level 4 autonomous shuttle, marking a significant milestone in the company’s expansion across Europe. This permit allows WeRide to conduct public road testing in Leuven, Belgium, in partnership with local authorities and transport operators. The testing is expected to lead to Belgium’s first commercial deployment of autonomous vehicles in mixed traffic, enhancing WeRide’s industry positioning as a leader in autonomous driving technology.

WeRide is a global leader in the autonomous driving industry and the first publicly traded Robotaxi company. WeRide is the only technology company with autonomous driving permits in seven markets, including China, Belgium, France, Singapore, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and the US. data.title: Fenbo Holdings Faces Nasdaq Compliance Challenge
data.description:
Fenbo Holdings Limited ( (FEBO) ) has shared an announcement.

On September 12, 2025, Fenbo Holdings Limited received a notification from Nasdaq indicating that its ordinary shares had fallen below the minimum bid price requirement of $1 for 30 consecutive business days. This notification does not immediately affect the company’s listing, as it has until March 11, 2026, to regain compliance. The company is exploring options to meet the requirement, including a potential reverse stock split if necessary. Failure to comply could lead to delisting, impacting its market presence and investor confidence.

Spark’s Take on FEBO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FEBO is a Neutral.

The absence of impactful earnings call insights leaves the stock outlook largely unchanged.

To see Spark’s full report on FEBO stock, click here.

More about Fenbo Holdings Limited

Fenbo Holdings Limited, established in 1993, initially operated as a toy manufacturer and distributor in Hong Kong. As the toy market declined, the company transitioned to manufacturing personal care electric appliances in 2005. It operates as an original equipment manufacturer for Spectrum Brands, producing electrical hair styling products under the ‘Remington’ brand. The company has a manufacturing subsidiary in Guangdong, PRC, capable of producing over three million units annually.

data.description:

On September 19, 2025, Jefferies Financial Group Inc. and Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation (SMBC) announced a significant expansion of their Global Strategic Alliance, initially formed in 2021 and expanded in 2023. data.title: Nordicus Partners Completes Unregistered Stock Offering
data.description:
Nordicus Partners ( (NORD) ) has shared an announcement.

Between July and September 2025, Nordicus Partners issued 1,057,500 restricted shares of common stock to 54 private investors at $1.90 per share. The offering was closed on September 18, 2025, and the shares were not registered under the Securities Act of 1933. The issuance was exempt from registration under Section 4(a)(2) and Rule 506(b) and (c) of Regulation D, as it was a private offering to accredited investors without public solicitation. No underwriters were involved, and the shares are subject to transfer restrictions.
